<h1><a class="h" name="Zephyr-Embedded-Controller-Zephyr-EC" href="#Zephyr-Embedded-Controller-Zephyr-EC"><span></span></a><a class="h" name="zephyr-embedded-controller-zephyr-ec" href="#zephyr-embedded-controller-zephyr-ec"><span></span></a>Zephyr Embedded Controller (Zephyr EC)</h1><div class="toc" role="navigation"><h2>Contents</h2><div class="toc-aux"><ul><li><a href="#Introduction">Introduction</a></li><li><a href="#Getting-Started">Getting Started</a></li><li><a href="#Source-Code-Organization">Source Code Organization</a></li><ul><li><a href="#Shared-Code">Shared Code</a></li><li><a href="#Zephyr-Subdirectory">Zephyr Subdirectory</a></li></ul></ul></div></div><h2><a class="h" name="Introduction" href="#Introduction"><span></span></a><a class="h" name="introduction" href="#introduction"><span></span></a>Introduction</h2><p>Starting roughly in July of 2021, Chromebooks switched from the original <a href="/chromiumos/platform/ec.git/+/HEAD/README.md">Google Chrome EC</a> to an application based on the <a href="https://zephyrproject.org/">Zephyr Project RTOS</a>.</p><p>The following terms are used to to describe these two implementations:</p><ul><li>Original Chrome EC: ECOS, cros-ec, and legacy EC</li><li>Zephyr-based EC: Zephyr EC</li></ul><h2><a class="h" name="Getting-Started" href="#Getting-Started"><span></span></a><a class="h" name="getting-started" href="#getting-started"><span></span></a>Getting Started</h2><p>The <a href="zephyr_build.md">Zephyr build</a> documentation details the usage of Google&#39;s meta build tool <code class="code">zmake</code>, used to configure and build Zephyr EC binaries. Start here if you only need to build a Zephyr EC binary for an existing board.</p><p>The <a href="project_config.md">project configuration</a> documentation details the steps required to create a new Chromebook program (reference board) and for creating a new project variant.</p><p>The <a href="zephyr_new_board_checklist.md">Zephyr New Board Checklist</a> links to the documentation needed to configure individual EC features.</p><p>The <a href="zephyr_troubleshooting.md">Zephyr Troubleshooting</a> page lists few common errors and troubleshooting techniques used when working with Zephyr.</p><h2><a class="h" name="Source-Code-Organization" href="#Source-Code-Organization"><span></span></a><a class="h" name="source-code-organization" href="#source-code-organization"><span></span></a>Source Code Organization</h2><p>Zephyr EC images rely on multiple Chromium repositories to build Zephyr EC images.</p><ul><li><a href="https://source.chromium.org/chromiumos/chromiumos/codesearch/+/main:src/third_party/zephyrproject/zephyr"><code class="code">third_party/zephyrproject/zephyr</code></a> - Google&#39;s local mirror of the main Zephyr RTOS source located on <a href="https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr">Github</a>.</li><li><a href="https://source.chromium.org/chromiumos/chromiumos/codesearch/+/main:src/platform/ec"><code class="code">platform/ec</code></a> - local repository containing code shared by the legacy EC and the Zephyr EC</li></ul><p>The current Zephyr EC builds only the CMSIS module from the upstream Zephyr source, but may use other modules in the future.</p><p>The diagram below shows the relationship of the upstream Zephyr repositories and the Chromium repositories.</p><p><img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Zephyr EC Structure" /></p><p>Google merges upstream Zephyr changes into the corresponding Chromium repository daily.</p><h3><a class="h" name="Shared-Code" href="#Shared-Code"><span></span></a><a class="h" name="shared-code" href="#shared-code"><span></span></a>Shared Code</h3><p>The two EC implementations currently share a significant amount code found in the following directories (all paths relative to the Chrome EC base directory <a href="https://source.chromium.org/chromiumos/chromiumos/codesearch/+/main:src/platform/ec"><code class="code">platform/ec</code></a>.)</p><ul><li><p><code class="code">common/</code> - Upper-level code shared across boards and the EC implementations. This includes the following features:</p><ul><li>Battery charging</li><li>USB Power Delivery</li><li>AP chipset power sequencing</li><li>Motionsense (EC sensor support)</li><li>Keyboard handling</li><li>Verified boot support</li></ul></li><li><p><code class="code">driver/</code> - Low-level drivers for on-board peripherals controlled by the EC. This does not include any drivers for modules integrated directly into the EC chipset (such as GPIO controllers, I2C, controllers, keyboard controller). On-board peripheral drivers include:</p><ul><li>Charge controllers</li><li>USB Power Delivery chips (TCPCs, PPCs, muxes, etc)</li><li>Temperature sensors</li><li>Motionsense sensors (accelerometers, gyrscopes, light sensors, etc)</li></ul></li><li><p><code class="code">include/</code> - Header files for the <code class="code">common/</code> and <code class="code">driver/</code> code</p></li></ul><p>The following legacy EC directories are not used by the Zephyr EC application:</p><ul><li><code class="code">baseboard/</code></li><li><code class="code">board/</code></li><li><code class="code">chip/</code></li><li><code class="code">core/</code></li></ul><h3><a class="h" name="Zephyr-Subdirectory" href="#Zephyr-Subdirectory"><span></span></a><a class="h" name="zephyr-subdirectory" href="#zephyr-subdirectory"><span></span></a>Zephyr Subdirectory</h3><p>Only the Zephyr EC application uses the following directory under the <a href="https://source.chromium.org/chromiumos/chromiumos/codesearch/+/main:src/platform/ec"><code class="code">platform/ec</code></a> repository:</p><ul><li><code class="code">zephyr/</code></li></ul><p>The following provides an overview of the sub-directories found under <code class="code">zephyr/</code>.</p><ul><li><code class="code">zephyr/app/</code> - The Zephyr EC application entry point. The Zephyr kernel calls <a href="https://source.chromium.org/chromiumos/chromiumos/codesearch/+/main:src/platform/ec/zephyr/app/ec/ec_app_main.c"><code class="code">ec_app_main()</code></a> routine after initializing all kernel services and chip-level drivers,</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/boards/</code> - Contains the EC chip-level support. This directory and the organization is required by the Zephyr build system.  This should not be confused with the legacy EC <code class="code">boards/</code> directory, as it does not contain any Chromebook specific board code.</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/cmake/</code> - Configuration files for the CMake build system.</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/drivers/</code> - Drivers conforming to the Zephyr device model to implement Chrome EC specific features. Google plans to eventually move these drivers upstream.</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/dts/</code> - Devicetree description files for Google&#39;s Zephyr drivers that are not yet available upstream.</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/emul/</code> - Emulator source code that has not yet been moved upstream.</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/include/</code> - Header files for files found in the <code class="code">zephyr/</code> sub-directory.</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/linker/</code> - Linker directive files used to construct the Zephyr EC binaries.</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/program/</code> - Program-specific configurations for each program supported by Zephyr.</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/shim/</code> - Source code that adapts the legacy EC APIs to the equivalent Zephyr OS API.</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/subsys/</code> - Staging area for subsystem code that will be moved upstream.</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/tests/</code> - Host based emulation tests.</li><li><code class="code">zephyr/zmake/</code> - Source code for the <code class="code">zmake/</code> meta tool.</li></ul>
